subject: Add support for using SSH
commit_message:
Add support for using SSH

This patch changes "create_config.py" script interface and its
behaviour:

* by default anonymous Gerrit is used for repository cloning and code
  URLS,
* external git mirror for code URLs might be provided but there is no
  default one,
* SSH credentials might be provided for repository cloning.

Cloning repository using SSH is required due to current ONAP
infrastructure HTTPS bandwidth constraints.

User wishing to use this setup should provide two environmental
variables:

1) API_USER - Gerrit username for cloning repositories via SSH
2) API_KEY - Gerrit API key (User menu -> Settings -> HTTP Credentials)

Be aware that a new RSA key will be generated and added to the user's
Gerrit account for purposes of Hound code search operation inside a VM.
Do remember to delete it (User menu -> Settings -> SSH keys) after
evaluating this environment.

Issue-ID: ONAPARC-540
Change-Id: I3e98dfa8582322d5b3bbbbf377d748faea0da57b
Signed-off-by: Pawel Wieczorek <p.wieczorek2@samsung.com>

entries.message:
Patch Set 1:

question(s) - is it feasible/possible to automate the removal of the new ssh key that gets added?
Any downside if the user forgets to delete the new key?

Patch Set 1: Code-Review+2

I am OK with the patch, regarding the unity of the set of associated patches
would it have made sense to create a dedicated repository under integration group?

Patch Set 1:

Could you please add a topic name for all commits in this relation chain? Would ease searching in future.

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> question(s) - is it feasible/possible to automate the removal of the new ssh key that gets added?

ID of the new key is returned on key addition ("seq" field of response [1]). If it were stored somewhere during provisioning, a trigger [2] for VM destroy procedure [3] could be provided which would use the ID to delete [4] said key.

I will provide a patch if you'd like me to. Let me know what you think

> Any downside if the user forgets to delete the new key?

There is no issue if the VM gets destroyed - the key would be removed too and only "SSH Keys" [5] section of evaluator's account would get messy. If the VM stays, it has the same privileges as the evaluators' account - hence the warning.

Code search on ONAP repositories could be made completely anonymous (with no need for user credentials), but current limitations of ONAP infra require using SSH for frequent fetches from origin repositories.

[1] https://gerrit-review.googlesource.com/Documentation/rest-api-accounts.html#add-ssh-key
[2] https://www.vagrantup.com/docs/triggers/
[3] https://www.vagrantup.com/docs/cli/destroy.html
[4] https://gerrit-review.googlesource.com/Documentation/rest-api-accounts.html#delete-ssh-key
[5] https://gerrit.onap.org/r/settings/#SSHKeys

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1: Code-Review+2
> 
> I am OK with the patch, regarding the unity of the set of associated patches
> would it have made sense to create a dedicated repository under integration group?

I am not sure. Currently components for bootstrapping ONAP environment are in fact decoupled. Having them in separate repositories might later bring some complexity (during testing or integration), though, if they become more dependent one on another.

Just to be clear: I am not an opponent of having separate repos (I would love to have e.g. granular Ansible roles for ONAP deployment available in Galaxy) but I am afraid that it might be too soon for splitting bootstrapping tools.

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> Could you please add a topic name for all commits in this relation chain? Would ease searching in future.

Pushing to refs/for/master%topic=code_search
Uploaded patch set 3: Patch Set 2 was rebased.
Change has been successfully merged by Morgan Richomme
